Short CV

Baris Metin graduated from Istanbul University, Cerrahpasa Medical Faculty as MD in 2004. Between 2004 and 2009 he completed neurology residency program at the same faculty. Then he started to work at Ghent University, Faculty of Psychology as a researcher and a PhD student. After getting his PhD in 2013, he continued at Ghent University as a postdoctoral researcher. From 2014 on, he has been working at Üsküdar University, Psychology Department. He is also the Research & Development Unit (PARGE) coordinator and the head of Technology Transfer Office. His research interests include ADHD, neuroimaging, experimental and cognitive psychology. He is teaching cognitive psychology for the undergraduate and postgraduate programs. In addition to that he gives consultancy in project development & management, patenting of intellectual properties, technology transfer and entrepreneurship.
